stories.title: Morning Light for Baby Hakeem
stories.preview: A gentle morning story about listening, gratitude, and tiny wonders.
content: The morning sky is soft and full of light, like a blanket woven with gold.
Baby Hakeem opens his eyes and hears the birds say, 'Bismillah' in their own bright way.
His mother smiles and says, 'Alhamdulillah' as she places his little hand on her heart.
In the garden, a caterpillar walks slowly. Hakeem watches and wonders: how does it move? How does it know where to go?
That is curiosity. Curiosity is the way we learn about Allah's beautiful creation.
stories.lesson: Good mornings begin with thankfulness, gentle curiosity, and a heart that notices small wonders.

dua.translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favour.

stories.title: The Evening Prayer and the Quiet Star
stories.preview: A calm evening story about thanking Allah and feeling safe under the stars.
content: The sky grows dark above the little house in Kumasi and the street lamps glow softly.
Baby Hakeem's family gathers to say their prayer together. His grandmother hums a quiet song.
She says, 'Every star is a promise from Allah. Every quiet breath is blessing.'
Hakeem thinks about the star he saw through the window when he woke from his nap.
Even when we are small, Allah hears us. Even when we are quiet, Allah is near.
stories.lesson: Evening is a time to remember Allah, to feel thankful, and to know that safety can be calm and gentle.

dua.translation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and wellbeing.

stories.title: Kwame's New Friend in London
stories.preview: A story about new places, friendly voices, and being proud of home.
content: Kwame walks through a green park in London. The air feels different from Accra, but the birds still sing.
He remembers how his grandfather drums at home. He remembers how his mother says, 'Your name is strong.'
A boy named Marcus waves and says, 'Good morning.' Kwame replies with a smile.
In the quiet of the park, Kwame learns that kindness can cross any country.
He feels grateful and says, 'Alhamdulillah' for a new friend and a new morning.
stories.lesson: Being kind and proud of your family helps you feel at home wherever you go.

dua.arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي
dua.transliteration: Rabbi ishrah li sadri wa yassir li amri
dua.translation: My Lord, expand my chest and ease my affairs.

stories.title: The Prayer Mat from Kumasi
stories.preview: Grandma teaches Ibrahim why prayer is a warm home inside the heart.
content: Ibrahim opens the quilted prayer mat that came from Kumasi. It smells like spices and home.
His grandma says, 'This mat remembers every prayer my feet touched. It remembers the rain and the mud.'
He closes his eyes and hears the soft hush of the room. The prayer mat makes the moment feel safe.
Grandma whispers, 'Allah listens no matter where you are. That is why prayer is easy when you trust Him.'
Ibrahim feels calm as the evening settles. He whispers his own thanks, and his heart feels like the mat — warm and steady.
stories.lesson: Prayer connects you to Allah even when the world around you changes.

dua.translation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favour.
